title: Split agora-cli into agora-agent library + thin frontends
description:
Refactor agora-cli to separate the protocol state machine from the TUI frontend. This enables multiple frontends (TUI, JMAP server, Flutter) to share the same user-agent core without duplicating logic.

Current problem: commands.rs (2,862 lines) mixes the protocol state machine (MLS, HPKE, directory, reconnect, payment watching) with TUI concerns (rustyline, ANSI coloring, slash command parsing, println). A second frontend (JMAP) cannot be added cleanly without this split.

Target workspace layout:
  agora-core           — unchanged
  agora-relay          — unchanged
  agora-agent          — NEW library crate: user agent machine
  agora-tui            — thin binary: terminal frontend
  agora-jmap           — thin binary: JMAP HTTP server (separate epic, depends on this)
  agora-flutter-bridge — updated to use agora-agent directly

agora-agent owns:
  - WebSocket connection + reconnect loop
  - Auth handshake (Ed25519 challenge/response, PoW)
  - MLS group state machine (key packages, Welcome, epoch tracking, HPKE re-derivation)
  - HPKE key management (publish, fetch, seal, unseal)
  - Directory state (online/offline peers, nicknames, aliases)
  - Local SQLite message store (history.rs, expanded)
  - Payment watch registry and lightwalletd integration

agora-agent exposes two async channel types to frontends:

  enum Command {
    Send { body: String },
    SendDm { to: String, body: String },
    SetNickname { name: String },
    SendFile { to: String, path: PathBuf },
    Subscribe { duration_days: u32 },
    GetDirectory,
    GetHistory { limit: usize },
    // ...
  }

  enum Event {
    MessageReceived { from: String, body: String, ts: i64 },
    UserJoined { pub_id: String, nickname: Option<String> },
    UserLeft { pub_id: String },
    DirectoryUpdate { online: Vec<UserInfo>, offline: Vec<UserInfo> },
    NicknameChanged { pub_id: String, nickname: String },
    PaymentConfirmed { session_id: String },
    Connected,
    Disconnected,
    // ...
  }

agora-tui becomes thin: rustyline reads stdin, slash commands map to Command, Events print to terminal. No protocol logic remains in the TUI.

Extraction work is primarily in commands.rs: everything inside the chat() event loop that is not println!/rustyline moves into agora-agent.
design: Channel-based interface between agent and frontends (Command in, Event out). Chosen over trait-based because it is simpler, naturally async, and maps cleanly to both TUI (stdin→Command, Event→print) and JMAP (HTTP request→Command, Event→EventSource push). Agent owns all state; frontends are stateless consumers.
notes: The main extraction work is the chat() function in cli/src/commands.rs. Everything that is not rustyline or println belongs in agora-agent. history.rs and profile.rs move with it. config.rs stays shared or moves to agora-agent.
